title: tephrosia decumbens
intro: Tephrosia decumbens, also known as the climbing hoofprint or creeping tephrosia, is a type of flowering plant in the pea family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Tephrosia decumbens include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
